note: U.S. Air Force. Assumed command 2025-08-15 (Stuttgart), succeeding Gen. Michael Langley (USMC); Senate-confirmed by voice vote 2025-07-31. First USAF officer to lead AFRICOM.
U.S. Navy. Assumed command 2025-08-08, succeeding Gen. Michael 'Erik' Kurilla (USA); first Navy officer to lead CENTCOM since 2008.

role: Commander, U.S. European Command (EUCOM) & Supreme Allied Commander Europe (SACEUR)
jurisdiction: U.S. European Command / NATO Allied Command Operations
note: U.S. Air Force. Assumed EUCOM 2025-07-01 and SACEUR 2025-07-04, succeeding Gen. Christopher Cavoli (USA).
U.S. Navy. 2024 holdover (assumed 2024-05-03), succeeding Adm. John Aquilino. DoD administratively rebranded the command 'U.S. Pacific Command' 2026-06-16; statutory name and leadership unchanged.

jurisdiction: U.S. Northern Command / North American Aerospace Defense Command
note: U.S. Air Force. 2024 holdover (assumed 2024-02-05), dual-hatted NORTHCOM + NORAD; succeeded Gen. Glen VanHerck.
U.S. Marine Corps. Senate-confirmed 2026-02-02; assumed command 2026-02-05. Succeeded acting cdr Lt. Gen. Evan Pettus (USAF) after Adm. Alvin Holsey retired early.
U.S. Space Force. 2024 holdover (assumed 2024-01-10). Gen. Michael Guetlein (who left for the Golden Dome program) was Vice Chief of Space Operations, not the SPACECOM commander; Whiting remains.
U.S. Navy. Assumed command 2025-12-05, succeeding Gen. Anthony J. Cotton (USAF); career submariner, previously STRATCOM deputy.
U.S. Air Force. 2024 holdover (assumed 2024-10-04), succeeding Gen. Jacqueline Van Ovost; 15th TRANSCOM commander.

role: Commander, U.S. Cyber Command (CYBERCOM) & Director, NSA
note: U.S. Army (career special operations). Senate-confirmed 2026-03-10 (71-29); dual-hatted CYBERCOM cdr + NSA director. Ended ~a year of acting leadership (Lt. Gen. William Hartman acting) after Gen. Timothy Haugh was removed April 2025.
U.S. Navy (SEAL). Senate-confirmed ~2025-08-01; assumed command 2025-10-03, succeeding Gen. Bryan Fenton (USA). Former JSOC commander.

jurisdiction: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
note: CHANGE since early 2025: Tulsi Gabbard (confirmed DNI 2025-02) announced resignation 2026-05-22 (effective 2026-06-19). Trump named Pulte acting DNI on 2026-06-02 (took over 2026-06-19), bypassing statutory successor PDDNI Aaron Lukas; Pulte concurrently remains FHFA Director with no prior intelligence background. Jay Clayton (SDNY U.S. Attorney) was nominated as permanent DNI 2026-06-11, pending/unconfirmed.

role: Principal Deputy Director of National Intelligence (PDDNI)
jurisdiction: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
note: Career intelligence officer. Senate-confirmed 2025-07-22 (51-46), sworn 2025-07-24. Briefly designated acting DNI on 2026-05-22 but Pulte was installed instead; Lukas continues as confirmed #2.

role: Director, National Security Agency (NSA) — dual-hat CYBERCOM
note: U.S. Army. Senate-confirmed 2026-03-10 (71-29); dual-hatted NSA Director + CYBERCOM commander (also listed as ccmd-cybercom).

person: Lt. Gen. James H. Adams III
note: U.S. Marine Corps. 25th DIA director; assumed 2026-02-20, succeeding Lt. Gen. Jeffrey Kruse (USAF).
U.S. Army. 9th NGA director; assumed 2025-11-05, succeeding Vice Adm. Frank Whitworth (USN, retired).
Career SES; NRO director since 2019, serving as lame-duck. Roger Mason nominated to succeed (SSCI hearing 2026-05-28; SASC hearing 2026-06-25); not yet Senate-confirmed as of as_of date.

role: Acting Assistant Secretary of State for Intelligence and Research (INR)
jurisdiction: U.S. Department of State, Bureau of Intelligence and Research
note: Career FSO serving in acting capacity since ~early 2025 (confirmed slot vacant after Brett Holmgren departed). CAVEAT: acting-holder identity rests on a single (Wikipedia) source and may be dated. Nominee Michael J. Vance placed on the Senate Executive Calendar 2026-06-23; not yet floor-confirmed.

role: Under Secretary for Intelligence and Analysis (DHS I&A)
jurisdiction: U.S. Department of Homeland Security, Office of Intelligence and Analysis
note: Political appointee; Senate-confirmed 2025-07-31. A proposed FY27 reorg would fold I&A into the DHS Secretary's office while keeping IC membership.

role: Assistant Secretary for Intelligence and Analysis (Treasury OIA)
jurisdiction: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Office of Intelligence and Analysis
note: Political appointee; Senate-confirmed 2025-12-18. Former SSCI staff, NSC and USMC officer.

role: Acting Director, Office of Intelligence and Counterintelligence (DOE)
jurisdiction: U.S. Department of Energy, Office of Intelligence and Counterintelligence
note: Career DOE-IN official, acting since ~early 2026 after Jay Tilden departed (position not Senate-confirmed). Exact acting-start date is a soft data point.

jurisdiction: U.S. Coast Guard, Coast Guard Intelligence
note: U.S. Coast Guard. In role since August 2022 (start day approximate); still listed as CG-2 in 2026 sources.

role: Deputy Chief of Space Operations for Intelligence (S2)
note: U.S. Space Force. Listed as S2 in the 2026 USAF/USSF Almanac. CONFLICT: one source (Wikipedia) reports Sidari departed ~March 2026 with civilian SES John J. Deeney IV serving as acting S2 — unverified against the Almanac; flagged for reconciliation.
U.S. Space Force. Senior ISR field commander; took command of Space Delta 7 (ISR) on 2024-06-13.

role: Deputy Chief of Staff, G-2 (Army Intelligence)
note: U.S. Army. Promoted to lieutenant general 2025-12-05 into the G-2 role; previously CG, 7th Infantry Division (to Oct 2025); succeeded Lt. Gen. Anthony Hale. Exact change-of-charge date approximate.

Civilian (retired Rear Adm.). Effective 2026-05-04 OPNAV N2N6 (DCNO for Information Warfare) was disestablished; Parode leads the new OPNAV Intelligence Division (N2) and is designated Acting Director, Naval Intelligence. Branch marked 'military' per category, but the current acting holder is a civilian.

role: Deputy Chief of Staff for Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance (A2)
note: U.S. Air Force. Assumed the intelligence-only A2 role ~2025-08 after the combined A2/6 was split (start day approximate).
U.S. Marine Corps. Assumed Director of Intelligence 2025-06-09 (promoted Maj. Gen. 2025-01-06).
Elevated from NCTC Deputy Director ~2026-03-18 after Director Joe Kent (confirmed 2025-07-30) resigned 2026-03-17 over U.S. entry into the Iran war.

role: U.S. Attorney, Southern District of New York
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Southern District of New York
note: AG-designated interim April 2025; court-appointed by SDNY judges August 2025. Former SEC chairman. Nominated by Trump to be DNI on 2026-06-11; remains SDNY U.S. Attorney pending confirmation.

role: U.S. Attorney, Eastern District of New York
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Eastern District of New York
note: AG-designated interim (sworn 2025-05-05); court-appointed by EDNY judges, sworn 2025-09-02. Senate nomination returned to the President 2026-01-03; continues via court appointment.

jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, District of Columbia
note: Senate-confirmed 2025-08-02 (sworn interim 2025-05-28), succeeding interim Ed Martin. Former NY judge/DA and Fox News host.

role: U.S. Attorney, Eastern District of Virginia
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Eastern District of Virginia
note: Contested/vacant as of as_of date. Erik Siebert (interim) resigned Sept 2025; Lindsey Halligan (interim) ruled unlawfully appointed Nov 2025 and left Jan 2026; court-appointed James W. Hundley (28 U.S.C. 546(d), 2026-02-20) was fired by DOJ 2026-02-21. A fresh court vacancy announcement was posted June 2026; no undisputed current holder identified.

role: U.S. Attorney, District of New Jersey
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, District of New Jersey
note: Career prosecutor (20+ yrs in the office); court-appointed (28 U.S.C. 546(d)) by Chief Judge Renee Bumb on 2026-03-23; appointment later ruled lawful. Replaced Alina Habba (resigned 2025-12-08 after courts disqualified her) and a subsequently disqualified interim trio.

role: U.S. Attorney, Northern District of Illinois
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Northern District of Illinois
note: AG 120-day interim (2025-04-07); court-appointed by N.D. Ill. judges effective 2025-08-05. Faced 2026 controversy over 'Broadview Six' dismissals and grand-jury practices; some members of Congress called for his resignation.

role: First Assistant U.S. Attorney performing U.S. Attorney duties, Central District of California
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Central District of California
note: Former Republican California assemblyman. Served as acting U.S. Attorney from July 2025; a court ruled he was unlawfully serving as 'acting' without Senate confirmation and barred that title, so he now performs U.S. Attorney duties as First Assistant U.S. Attorney.

role: U.S. Attorney, Southern District of Florida
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Southern District of Florida
note: Senate-confirmed 2025-08-02; sworn 2025-08-13. First U.S. Attorney Senate-confirmed in Trump's second term. Former state judge and Army reservist; succeeded interim Hayden O'Byrne.

jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, District of Massachusetts
note: Career federal prosecutor (23 yrs). Appointed interim by the Acting AG 2025-01-20; subsequently continued (court-appointed) as U.S. Attorney.

role: U.S. Attorney, Northern District of California
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, Northern District of California
note: Career prosecutor; sworn in May 2025 (interim/court-appointed). Continues to serve in 2026.

All 93 U.S. Attorney districts exist; only the highest-profile offices are individually enumerated here. Many 2025-2026 U.S. Attorney posts are interim/acting (AG 120-day designations, court appointments under 28 U.S.C. 546(d), or First Assistants serving as acting), and several have been the subject of court disqualification fights.
